name: Ensure Function App runtime version is currently supported
description: Function Apps with deprecated runtime stacks or unsupported host runtime versions
timedControlSummary: Unsupported Azure Functions runtimes should be migrated before provider support and platform guarantees end.
timedControlDisruptionRisk: Late runtime migrations can block deployments or require urgent code and dependency changes.
body:
Function App runtime stacks and Azure Functions host versions eventually go out of support as language versions and platform images age. Unsupported runtimes can carry unpatched vulnerabilities, may block platform upgrades, and often prevent teams from consuming current security features.

This control checks both provider-marked deprecated language stacks and Function Apps pinned to old host runtimes such as `~1`, `~2`, or `~3`. Runtime upgrades should be handled as a release-management activity: validate code compatibility, update dependencies, redeploy, and monitor the new version.
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

Move the Function App to Azure Functions runtime `~4` and a supported language stack. The exact command depends on the operating system and language stack, so validate the target runtime in a staging slot or non-production app first.

### Azure CLI

Inspect the current host runtime setting:

```bash
az functionapp config appsettings list \
  --resource-group {{asset.azureResourceGroup}} \
  --name {{asset.name}} \
  --query "[?name=='FUNCTIONS_EXTENSION_VERSION'].{name:name,value:value}"
```

Inspect the current language runtime settings:

```bash
az functionapp config show \
  --resource-group {{asset.azureResourceGroup}} \
  --name {{asset.name}} \
  --query '{linuxFxVersion:linuxFxVersion,nodeVersion:nodeVersion,pythonVersion:pythonVersion,javaVersion:javaVersion,powerShellVersion:powerShellVersion,netFrameworkVersion:netFrameworkVersion}'
```

Set the Azure Functions host runtime to `~4`:

```bash
az functionapp config appsettings set \
  --resource-group {{asset.azureResourceGroup}} \
  --name {{asset.name}} \
  --settings FUNCTIONS_EXTENSION_VERSION=~4
```

For Linux Function Apps, set the target runtime stack. Replace `{{manual.linuxFxVersion}}` with the approved stack value, for example a currently supported `PYTHON|...`, `NODE|...`, `DOTNET-ISOLATED|...`, or `JAVA|...` value.

```bash
az functionapp config set \
  --resource-group {{asset.azureResourceGroup}} \
  --name {{asset.name}} \
  --linux-fx-version "{{manual.linuxFxVersion}}"
```

For Windows Function Apps, update the language-specific setting supported by your stack and deployment model, then redeploy the function package from CI/CD.

### Rollout guidance

1. Update application dependencies and build images/packages against the target runtime.
2. Use deployment slots for production Function Apps and swap only after health checks pass.
3. Validate triggers, bindings, managed identity access, and outbound dependencies after redeploy.
4. Add CI/CD checks so unsupported runtime stack values cannot be reintroduced.

### References

- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-functions/functions-versions
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-functions/supported-languages
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/functionapp/config

timedControlRules.recommendedTarget: Set FUNCTIONS_EXTENSION_VERSION to ~4 and migrate the app to Azure Functions runtime 4.x.
timedControlRules.summary: Azure Functions host runtimes 1.x, 2.x, and 3.x should be migrated to runtime 4.x.
timedControlRules.disruptionRisk: Old Functions host runtimes are unsupported or nearing provider support deadlines and can lose platform guarantees.

timedControlRules.recommendedTarget: Set Node.js runtime to 20 or newer on Azure Functions v4.
timedControlRules.summary: Unsupported Azure Functions runtimes should be migrated before provider support and platform guarantees end.
timedControlRules.disruptionRisk: Late runtime migrations can block deployments or require urgent code and dependency changes.

timedControlRules.recommendedTarget: Set Python runtime to 3.12 on Azure Functions v4.
timedControlRules.summary: Unsupported Azure Functions runtimes should be migrated before provider support and platform guarantees end.
timedControlRules.disruptionRisk: Late runtime migrations can block deployments or require urgent code and dependency changes.

timedControlRules.recommendedTarget: Set Java runtime to 17 or 21 on Azure Functions v4.
timedControlRules.summary: Unsupported Azure Functions runtimes should be migrated before provider support and platform guarantees end.
timedControlRules.disruptionRisk: Late runtime migrations can block deployments or require urgent code and dependency changes.

timedControlRules.recommendedTarget: Set PowerShell runtime to 7.4 on Azure Functions v4.
timedControlRules.summary: Unsupported Azure Functions runtimes should be migrated before provider support and platform guarantees end.
timedControlRules.disruptionRisk: Late runtime migrations can block deployments or require urgent code and dependency changes.

timedControlRules.recommendedTarget: Set runtime to .NET 8 isolated worker on Azure Functions v4.
timedControlRules.summary: Unsupported Azure Functions runtimes should be migrated before provider support and platform guarantees end.
timedControlRules.disruptionRisk: Late runtime migrations can block deployments or require urgent code and dependency changes.

timedControlRules.recommendedTarget: Set Linux worker runtime to a supported Azure Functions v4 stack, for example NODE|20, PYTHON|3.12, JAVA|21, or DOTNET-ISOLATED|8.0.
timedControlRules.summary: Unsupported Azure Functions runtimes should be migrated before provider support and platform guarantees end.
timedControlRules.disruptionRisk: Late runtime migrations can block deployments or require urgent code and dependency changes.
